title: festuca rubra subsp. heterophylla
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. heterophylla is a subspecies of grass known as red fescue, a perennial cool-season grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. heterophylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
